attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Paces

### rbrr-depot-identity-collapse (₢BBAAM) [rough]

## Character

Cycle-driven debug. The five-step depot-identity-collapse implementation landed
(commits ee816373 → d2dec554). Remaining work is verifying it against live GCP
by stepping through what RELEASE.md *would* prescribe — except this pace does
not have the full RELEASE.md tabtarget set yet (canonical entry point `rbw-tP`
is unbuilt; step-3 inner sequence beyond the pristine-lifecycle fixture is
unreachable). Cycles run against the buildable subset.

Load-bearing discipline: each turn does exactly one cycle — execute the next
reachable step, surface what fails, diagnose root cause, recommend fix shape,
then stop. No auto-fix. The stop keeps the operator in the loop and prevents
the drift where mechanical fixes paper over architectural questions.

## Scope

- Step through reachable RELEASE.md-equivalent today: Payor health probe →
  marshal-zero + commit → pristine-lifecycle fixture (cases 1, 2, 3).
- Out-of-reach this pace: canonical infrastructure setup, supply chain
  (inscribe/ordain), Crucible suite, cleanup. Those become reachable once
  `rbw-tP` is drafted in a follow-on pace.
- Per surfaced issue, route disposition: notch into BBAAM if in-scope-blocking
  the verification path, slate as separate pace if structural or out-of-scope,
  capture as itch if speculative.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract (waived to main for
  initial debug per operator).
- No fix lands without explicit operator nod after diagnosis is described.
- Diagnosed-but-unfixed issues accumulate as scar candidates for wrap-time
  routing.

### cult-verb-foundation (₢BBAAN) [rough]

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
- Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

### handbook-cult-verb-sweep (₢BBAAR) [rough]

## Character

Operator-handbook sweep for the cult-verb refactor (BBAAN) and the producer-output discipline. Lands after BBAAN so handbook narrative describes settled vocabulary, not intent. Designed to run in parallel with downstream coding paces — the handbook surface is operator-facing prose, not depot runtime code, so it doesn't block any depot/foundry work.

Different cognitive posture from the coding paces it reflects — prose with intentional ceremony tone. Best held in a separate session from the coding officium, parallel to BBAAQ. Soft pairing with BBAAQ for vocabulary consistency between spec and operator narrative.

## Scope

Five handbook files in `Tools/rbk/rbh0/`, all with paddock-settled vocabulary by the time this pace mounts.

**rbhogw_governor_wrapper.sh — primary surface.** The canonical operator narrative for SA management.
- Cult-verb constant refs: `RBYC_CHARTER`, `RBYC_KNIGHT`, `RBZ_CHARTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_KNIGHT_DIRECTOR`, `RBZ_LIST_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS` — adapt to whatever BBAAN settles.
- Verb-as-noun ceremony lines (L63, L66, L73): "Create a Retriever with read access (Charter):", "Create a Director with build access (Knight):", "List issued service accounts:" — adjust to the new vocabulary.
- Producer-output narrative (L49-52): if BBAAN's assay-slot-exclusive credential-write changes the operator's RBRA install path, update the install-step instructions accordingly.

**rbhocr_credential_retriever.sh, rbhocd_credential_director.sh — credential install.** Light verb touch-ups in narrative; potential producer-output flow adjustment via the shared `zrbho_credential_install` helper. `RBYC_TALLY` reference in rbhocr (L42) — adjust if tally vocabulary moves.

**rbhopw_payor_wrapper.sh — payor wrapper.** `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` reference (L95) — confirm mantle stays unchanged per BBAAN's cult-verb decision (mantle stays as singleton-per-depot exception). Producer-output narrative for governor RBRA placement parallels rbhogw.

**rbhob_base.sh — shared helpers.** `zrbho_credential_install` may need adjustment if BBAAN settles assay-slot-exclusive write semantics — base helper changes ripple consistently across all credential install narratives.

## Operator-visible behaviors from BBAAN

These behaviors must surface in narrative; the handbook is the operator's contract.

**Divest removes the production RBRA.** After successful cloud DELETE, the role's local production RBRA file is also removed. Operator narrative should state this explicitly so the disappearing file isn't a surprise.

**Invest is fatal if the SA already exists.** Re-keying flow is divest-then-invest. Narrative should guide the operator to divest first when re-investing.

**Unmake silently absorbs governor cleanup.** No separate governor forfeit step in the procedure. Operator behavior is unchanged ("just run unmake"); the absence-of-mantle case is invisible to the operator (404-tolerant under the hood).

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Handbook narrative describes what landed in BBAAN — read the actual code state, do not work from intent. If implementation differs from paddock-settled vocabulary, raise it; do not silently reconcile.
- Preserve the existing operator-narrative tone — ceremony language is intentional. Verb swaps should keep the same cadence (e.g., the parenthetical "(Charter):" pattern stays as "(Invest):", not flattened into prose).
- Use existing `buh_line` / `buh_step` / `buh_section` / `buh_tt` primitives — no narrative-structure rework.
- Real-time review at each step before commit. No pre-engineered choices.

## Out of scope

- Code logic changes beyond what's required to consume renamed RBZ/RBYC constants. Structural changes for new producer-output discipline land in BBAAN; this pace adapts narrative to whatever BBAAN settled.
- Handbook files outside the five listed — depot-identity narrative is already swept by BBAAM Step 1; non-SA handbooks (crucible, windows, tadmor, first-crucible, dispatch CLI) don't touch the changing surfaces.
- Spec/README/RBYC vocabulary documentation — owned by sibling pace ₢BBAAQ.
- Restructuring handbook section ordering or step decomposition — out of scope for a vocabulary sweep.
- New onboarding content — out of scope.

### phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed (₢BBAAK) [rough]

## Character

Theurge consumer pace. Rebuilds pristine-lifecycle fixture atop the new SA-management surface produced by the cult-verb foundation pace (BBAAN). Depends on BBAAN landing first. Character is mechanical — the new shape is settled in the paddock; this pace adapts theurge to it. Intent-only docket — code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state, not pre-staged here.

## Scope

Pristine-lifecycle section restructures. BBAAJ's §2 (pristine-lifecycle-simple) and BBAAK's original §3 (pristine-lifecycle-credentialed) collapse into a single unified section containing three fused cases that share one throwaway depot for the run:

- Depot stand-up — depot levy and the rbrr field install needed to satisfy downstream preconditions.
- SA cycle — governor mantle, retriever invest, director invest, access-probes, divests of operational SAs.
- Depot tear-down — unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup.

Test consumes per-row observation facts via type-extension lookup. All stdout-substring assertions retire from pristine-lifecycle code. Test-side primitives reduce to tabtarget invocation, file copy (assay → role production path), `Path::exists()` on facts, and one fact-read for depot project_id.

Manifest registration: add new cult-verb colophons; remove the colophons retired by BBAAN.

Cost reduction: one throwaway depot per fixture run, down from four under the original BBAAJ+BBAAK design.

## BBAAN sequencing implications

These flow from BBAAN's settled implementation conventions and constrain the fused-case ordering.

**Post-divest production RBRA absence.** "Divests of operational SAs" leaves no production RBRA at the role's path (BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA decision). Any subsequent fixture step that needs those credentials must re-invest first. The current SA-cycle ordering (mantle → invest → access-probes → divest) is correct as written; tear-down does not need credentials.

**Invest fatal-on-exists.** Re-invest within a single fixture run requires divest first (BBAAN's fatal-if-exists decision). The fused-case design already lands invest exactly once per role; no in-run re-invest needed.

**Governor cleanup folded into unmake.** No separate "forfeit governor" step appears in the fused case — `Depot tear-down — unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup` is the entire cleanup. The 404-tolerant unmake makes ordering robust to mid-fixture failures (an unmake after a failed mantle still cleans up cleanly).

**RBCC fact-extension constants.** BBAAN consolidates fact-extension tinder constants into one contiguous block in RBCC (migrating `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC; new retriever/director ext constants join it). Rust-side consumers — including `RBTDRP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and any new fact-name imports — must point to the RBCC home. Pristine fixture is currently service-tier-broken pending BBAAM Step 5; this remap is part of the bring-back-online work for this pace.

## Constraints

- Depends on BBAAN landing first.
- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract: stop on first failure cleanly; commits during the run are first-class; no recovery scaffolding.
- Reuse paddock-locked patterns; do not re-engineer them here.
- Real-time review of each step before commit. Prior-Claude does not pre-engineer for current-Claude.

## Out of scope

- SA-management surface refactor (BBAAN).
- Phase 1+ of rbw-tP (BBAAC and onward).
- Multi-instance test scenarios beyond single-instance per role.
- Case 1 (marshal-zero gate) — stays in §1, untouched by this rebuild.

### phase-1-depot-lifecycle (₢BBAAC) [rough]

## Character

Creates the persistent cloud infrastructure used by all downstream phases of `rbw-tP`. Runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture (BBAAB) gates and the lifecycle exercises pass. This is the "real" mantling/investing plus the "real" canonical depot levy — the state that persists for reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs.

Quota-aware: canonical depot creation is one of the daily quota draws (the other being the throwaway depot in BBAAB's depot-lifecycle case). Two GCP projects per pristine run total.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so the new colophons are available.

### cult-verb-foundation (₢BBAAN) [rough]

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).

## BBAAN sequencing implications

These flow from BBAAN's settled implementation conventions and constrain the fused-case ordering.

**Post-divest production RBRA absence.** "Divests of operational SAs" leaves no production RBRA at the role's path (BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA decision). The current SA-cycle ordering (mantle → invest → access-probes → divest) is correct as written; tear-down does not need credentials.

**Invest fatal-on-exists.** Re-invest within a single fixture run requires divest first (BBAAN's fatal-if-exists decision). The fused-case design already lands invest exactly once per role; no in-run re-invest needed.

**Governor cleanup folded into unmake.** No separate "forfeit governor" step appears in the fused case — `Depot tear-down — unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup` is the entire cleanup. The 404-tolerant unmake makes ordering robust to mid-fixture failures (an unmake after a failed mantle still cleans up cleanly).

**RBCC fact-extension constants.** BBAAN consolidates fact-extension tinder constants into one contiguous block in RBCC (migrating `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC; new retriever/director ext constants join it). Rust-side consumers — including `RBTDRP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and any new fact-name imports — must point to the RBCC home. I've groomed BB. Heat is `rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification`, racing, 13 remaining paces.

## Strictly spec-only — safe in parallel

**₢BBAAV spec-dangling-xref-sweep** — Purely `.adoc`. Character explicitly says: *"No code, no narrative tracks — pure .adoc"* and *"Runs in parallel with any non-spec work."* Mechanical xref flipping (`{rbtgo_director_knight}` → `{rbtgo_director_invest}`, etc.) plus an orphan `.adoc` keep-or-`git rm` decision. **Highest-confidence parallel pace.**

**₢BBAAI release-doc-revision** — Pure RELEASE.md + README.md cross-doc consistency sweep. Spec/doc-only, but character marks it as a *maintenance pace* deliberately scheduled AFTER the rest of the heat lands (it re-reads RELEASE.md "with fresh eyes" and applies corrections accumulated during BBAAB–BBAAH execution). Spec-only, but **not parallel-with-everything** — it consumes the work the other paces produce.

## Adjacent — narrative-bash, not strictly spec

**₢BBAAR handbook-cult-verb-sweep** — Handbook `.sh` files (rbhogw, rbhocr, rbhocd, rbhopw, rbhob) that orchestrate operator-facing prose via `buh_*` primitives. Character says: *"the handbook surface is operator-facing prose, not depot runtime code, so it doesn't block any depot/foundry work"* and explicitly designed *"to run in parallel with downstream coding paces."* But it IS `.sh` code that consumes BBAAN's renamed `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constants — depends on BBAAN landing first, then runs parallel to BBAAK/BBAAC/BBAAE/etc.

## Not spec-only (for completeness)

BBAAM (live GCP cycle-debug), BBAAN (coding-only foundation), BBAAU (Bash + Rust + tabtarget rename), BBAAP (test-code audit), BBAAK (theurge consumer), BBAAC/BBAAE/BBAAF/BBAAG (rbw-tP phase implementations), BBAAH (slash command + artifact wiring).

## Suggested parallel posture

If you want one spec officium going alongside coding work right now: **mount BBAAV in a separate session.** It has no dependencies on in-flight paces (BBAAQ already landed Surface 2/4 retirements), and its character explicitly authorizes parallelism. BBAAR can run alongside coding paces too, but only after BBAAN lands. BBAAI waits for the heat tail.

# Release Procedure

The release qualification ceremony for the project maintainer — five operator steps, roughly one hour wall-clock, with cloud cost on the order of two GCP projects per run.
The ceremony exists to catch silent first-build assumptions that the routine `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` and `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` tiers tolerate by design.

[Payor](README.md#Payor) OAuth is the only prerequisite credential.
All other credentials — [Governor](README.md#Governor), [Director](README.md#Director), and [Retriever](README.md#Retriever) service-account [RBRA](README.md#RBRA) files — are minted by the qualification itself, not restored from backup.
The entire credential chain must be reproducible from a clean local state.

**Failure mode contract.** Mid-qualification failure means start over from step 2, not patch-and-continue.
Patching forward is exactly the bug class this ceremony exists to catch — accumulated state hides first-build assumptions.

## 1. Confirm Payor health

- [Refreshes](README.md#Refresh) the [Payor](README.md#Payor) OAuth token so subsequent [Payor](README.md#Payor)-authority operations do not fail mid-qualification on token expiry
- Use `tt/rbw-gPI.PayorInstall.sh` instead if [Payor](README.md#Payor) credentials have never been [Installed](README.md#Install) on this workstation
- Success: the command exits clean
- Failure: re-[Establish](README.md#Establish) the [Manor](README.md#Manor) before proceeding — the ceremony cannot run without a healthy [Payor](README.md#Payor)

```
tt/rbw-gPR.PayorRefresh.sh
```

## 2. Marshal zero, then commit

The marshal-zero operation returns local state to a blank template:

- Blanks [Repo Regime](README.md#RBRR) fields ([Depot](README.md#Depot) identity, project ID, prefixes)
- Deletes all [RBRA](README.md#RBRA) credential files ([Governor](README.md#Governor), [Director](README.md#Director), [Retriever](README.md#Retriever))
- Blanks [Hallmark](README.md#Hallmark) pins in each [Nameplate](README.md#Nameplate) regime file ([RBRN](README.md#RBRN))
- Blanks depot-scoped fields in each [Vessel](README.md#Vessel) regime file ([RBRV](README.md#RBRV))

Then commit the result with the marshal-zero signature in the commit message — step 3 detects that signature on `HEAD` and refuses to run otherwise.

- Success: working tree clean and `HEAD` carries the signature
- Failure: review the marshal output for files the operation would not zero, resolve, and retry

```
tt/rbw-MZ.MarshalZeroes.sh
```

## 3. Run the pristine qualification

The release gate.

**Entry contract** — `rbw-tP` refuses to run unless ALL of the following hold:

- marshal-zero signature on `HEAD`
- working tree clean
- [RBRA](README.md#RBRA) files absent
- [Repo Regime](README.md#RBRR) fields blank
- [Hallmark](README.md#Hallmark) pins blank in every [Nameplate](README.md#Nameplate) regime ([RBRN](README.md#RBRN))
- depot-scoped fields blank in every [Vessel](README.md#Vessel) regime file ([RBRV](README.md#RBRV))

The contract is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline — that property is what lets this tier catch silent-first-build assumptions by construction.

**Cost** — ~1 hour wall-clock plus two GCP projects per run:

- one throwaway [Depot](README.md#Depot) created and torn down by the `pristine-lifecycle` fixture's `depot-lifecycle` case
- one canonical [Depot](README.md#Depot) [Levied](README.md#Levy) by the canonical-infrastructure setup phase
- GCP project deletion is asynchronous (30-day soft delete) — each run leaves pending-delete projects in the project list; this is accepted cost

**Sequence**:

- `pristine-lifecycle` fixture — marshal-zero gate plus idempotent throwaway lifecycles for [Depot](README.md#Depot), [Governor](README.md#Governor), [Retriever](README.md#Retriever), and [Director](README.md#Director)
- canonical infrastructure setup — [Mantle](README.md#Mantle) the [Governor](README.md#Governor) + deploy [RBRA](README.md#RBRA), [Charter](README.md#Charter) the [Retriever](README.md#Retriever) + deploy [RBRA](README.md#RBRA), [Knight](README.md#Knight) the [Director](README.md#Director) + deploy [RBRA](README.md#RBRA), [Levy](README.md#Levy) the canonical [Depot](README.md#Depot)
- supply chain — inscribe [Reliquary](README.md#Reliquary), [Ordain](README.md#Ordain) all [Hallmarks](README.md#Hallmark)
- [Crucible](README.md#Crucible) suite
- cleanup

Outcome:

- Success: green tally and clean exit
- Failure: return to step 2 — never patch-and-continue

```
tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh
```

## 4. Prepare the upstream release

- Claude Code slash command, not a tabtarget — the contribution ceremony is interactive by design
- Drives the upstream contribution review and produces the contribution-ready branch
- Success: the ceremony reports a clean upstream-ready state
- Failure: address findings and re-run; if the underlying issue is structural, return to step 2

```
/rbk-prep-release
```

## 5. Push to the upstream remote

- Universal git operation; no tabtarget
- Pushes the contribution-ready branch produced by step 4
- Success: the upstream accepts the push
- Failure: resolve the upstream-side reason (branch protection, force-push policy, etc.) and retry — the local state from step 4 remains valid

```
git push <upstream> <branch>
```

content.text:
Oriented. Here's what I found.

## The phase/coronet collision

`rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh` **does not exist as a tabtarget.** It's named in RELEASE.md §3 (line 77) and referenced in BBAAM's docket as "the canonical entry point" — but the file isn't there. Five of the remaining paces (BBAAC, BBAAE, BBAAF, BBAAG, BBAAH) all describe "Add Phase N to `rbw-tP`." They're sub-construction of one unbuilt orchestrator script, treated as if each phase were a coherent deliverable.

That's the prior-LLM error you're sniffing: **the "phases" are an internal-script axis being promoted to a planning axis.** Coronets are how we track work. RELEASE.md §3 already lists the script's sequence as a flat bullet list (pristine-lifecycle fixture → canonical setup → supply chain → crucible → cleanup). That bullet list IS the phases. We don't need it shadow-encoded as five paces.

Also: "phase" is already polysemous in the codebase — `rbq_Qualify.sh` uses Phase 1/2/3 for qualification tiers, `rbhw0_top.sh` uses Phase 1–4 for Windows handbook setup, RBSGS uses Phase 1/2 for Establish/Setup. Adding a *sixth* phase axis under rbw-tP is collision-on-collision.

## What already has variants in the codebase

Each phase-pace's "work" list is mostly **one-line invocations of existing tabtargets**:

| Pace | "Work" items | Already exists? |
|------|--------------|-----------------|
| BBAAC phase-1-depot-lifecycle | levy depot, mantle gov, invest retr/dir, roster, IAM wait | `rbw-dL`, `rbw-aM`, BBAAN-future `rbw-arI/adI/arr/adr` — all tabtargets. The throwaway flavor is already inside `rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle` and `rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle` in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs:558,699` |
| BBAAE phase-3-reliquary-and-hallmark | inscribe, conjure/bind/graft, vouch, summon, plumb, tally, rekon | `rbw-dI`, `rbw-fO`, `rbw-fk`, `rbw-fV`, `rbw-fs`, `rbw-fpc/pf`, `rbw-ft`, `rbw-irh/r` — all exist today |
| BBAAF phase-4-crucible-suite | charge/run/quench × {tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml} | `rbw-cC`, `rbw-cQ`, `rbtd-r.Run.{tadmor,moriah,srjcl,pluml}.sh` — all exist today |
| BBAAG phase-5-cleanup | abjure, divest, unmake, tally | `rbw-fA`, BBAAN-future `rbw-arD/adD`, `rbw-dU`, `rbw-irh` — all tabtargets. Plus the "first end-to-end run" — that's an *event*, not a deliverable |
| BBAAH phase-6-prep-release | modify `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` + tiny artifact write in `rbw-tP` | Genuinely new (this one's fine as-is — it's one file plus a sentinel) |

The sub-decomposition under "Phase 0" tells a different story:

| Pace | What | Already exists? |
|------|------|-----------------|
| BBAAB phase-0-pristine-preflight-detector | preflight gate logic | **Already landed** — `rbtdrp_marshal_zero_attestation` at `rbtdrp_pristine.rs:490`, registered as `pristine-lifecycle-gate` section |
| BBAAJ phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-simple | depot + governor lifecycle | **Already landed** — `rbtdrp_depot_lifecycle` (L558), `rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle` (L699), registered as `pristine-lifecycle-simple` section |
| BBAAK phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed | rebuild + add SA cycle (fused case) | **Genuinely remaining** — the fixture has only gate + simple sections today; this pace adds the credentialed/SA-cycle cases atop BBAAN |

So Phase 0's three coronets each map to discrete fixture sections in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. **That decomposition matches a real code seam.** The Phase 1+ coronets don't.

## My recommendation

**Collapse BBAAC, BBAAE, BBAAF, BBAAG into ONE pace** — `rbw-tP-orchestrator-script` (or similar). The deliverable is "write the bash file that calls the existing tabtargets in the order RELEASE.md §3 specifies." That's a single coherent unit of work. The "phases" become section comments inside one bash script. RELEASE.md §3 is already the spec.

**Keep BBAAH separate** — it touches a different file (`rbk-prep-release.md`) plus a sentinel artifact, and its work depends on rbw-tP existing first. Genuine successor pace, not a phase.

**Keep BBAAK as-is** — it's real fixture-section work, properly sized.

**Rename to drop "phase"** — even the survivors shouldn't carry "phase-N" silks given the collision with rbq Phase 1/2/3. Something like `rbw-tP-build` for the orchestrator and `pristine-credentialed-cases` for BBAAK keeps domain language without inventing a parallel ordinal scheme.

Want me to draft the merged docket for the collapse, or do you want to think on the framing first?

message.content: Yes, shortly we will collapse as per your recommendation.  I wonder that we ought to have a 'coding' pace distinct from a 'testing' pace though to make sure we wirk the issues?

content.text:
Yes, but I'd reframe the seam slightly.

"Coding vs testing" isn't quite right — many bugs in the orchestrator surface during construction itself (wrong tabtarget args, missing plumbing, structural errors). What's genuinely separable is **construction** (cheap iteration, mechanical) vs **first fresh-state run against real cloud** (~1hr wall-clock, 2 GCP projects, one-shot ceremony where silent first-build assumptions surface — the bug class this whole heat exists to catch).

That seam is load-bearing because:
- **Cost asymmetry.** Construction is free; the first run is the most expensive event in the heat. Tying them in one pace conflates cheap-iteration commits with the ceremonial single-shot run.
- **Posture asymmetry.** Construction is "did I wire the calls correctly?" — verifiable by reading the script. The first run is "what does fresh-state reality teach us?" — investigative, surface surprises, route them.
- **Discovery routing.** Surprises during the first run want a separate landing pad — fix-inline vs spawn-follow-up decisions live there, not buried in a construction commit.

Notably, BBAAG already had this instinct half-baked: "Cleanup phase plus the actual first run." The first-run-as-discovery was architected into phase-5 originally. My recommendation extracts it cleanly.

So the proposed shape:

| Pace | Character | Cost |
|------|-----------|------|
| **rbw-tP construction** (collapsed BBAAC+E+F+G-cleanup) | Write the orchestrator. Calls existing tabtargets per RELEASE.md §3. Includes cleanup logic. Mechanical. | Free |
| **rbw-tP first-run** (extracted from BBAAG) | One end-to-end run against fresh state. Surface + route surprises. Inline tiny fixes; spawn follow-up paces for big ones. Capture wall-clock + cost for capacity planning. | ~1hr + 2 projects |
| **BBAAH prep-release integration** | Wires `/rbk-prep-release` to require pristine pass. Depends on first-run having happened (else nothing to gate on). | Free |
| **BBAAI release-doc-revision** | Cold re-read of RELEASE.md after everything lands. | Free |

One refinement worth weighing: the first-run pace is genuinely *informative* rather than *fail-blocking* (per BBAAG's original framing). That suggests its docket Character should explicitly say "wrap on completion regardless of green/red — findings are the deliverable, not the green tally." Otherwise we'll relitigate "do we wrap if a surprise blocked the run?" mid-execution.

Sound right, or do you want to push back on the split?

message.content: You're splitting hairs.  Suggest effective sequence of engineering without adding confusion eddies.

content.text:
Fair. Drop the split.

**One pace** builds `rbw-tP` and runs it end-to-end. The script is just calls to existing tabtargets — testing it IS running it. Surprises during execution get notched or routed to follow-ups like in any other pace. That's not a separate pace, that's normal pace discipline.

Final shape:

1. **BBAAK** phase-0-fixture-lifecycle-credentialed — fixture sections, real seam, keep
2. **rbw-tP-build-and-run** (collapses BBAAC + BBAAE + BBAAF + BBAAG) — write the orchestrator, run it, capture findings
3. **BBAAH** prep-release-integration — wire `/rbk-prep-release` to require pristine pass
4. **BBAAI** release-doc-revision — cold re-read of RELEASE.md after everything lands

Plus the spec/handbook parallel-safe paces from earlier (BBAAV, BBAAR) and the in-flight foundation work (BBAAM, BBAAN, BBAAU, BBAAP). Ready to draft the merged docket when you are.

message.content: I've noticed in the past that you often drive a wrap at completion of construction rather than at 'all tests actually pass', which is why I split into 2 paces.

content.text:
You're right. That's a real pattern in my behavior, and the structural split is a guardrail against it — not hair-splitting. The wrap-on-construction-complete drift is exactly the failure mode the two-pace shape prevents.

# jjezs_slate rbw-tP-build

## Character

Construction of the canonical entry-point script `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`. Stitches existing tabtargets per RELEASE.md §3 sequence into one orchestrator. No new cloud-mutating logic — every cloud step is a call to an existing tabtarget. The script's value is sequence, entry-contract enforcement, and exit gating, not novel functionality.

Coding-only pace. **Wrap criterion**: script exists, parses cleanly, each tabtarget invocation has the right shape under cheap dry-checks. The gate-on-green discipline lives in the sibling first-run pace.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time.

## Scope

New file: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`. Body comprises three responsibilities.

**Entry contract** (per RELEASE.md L46–53): refuse to run unless ALL hold:
- marshal-zero signature on HEAD
- working tree clean
- RBRA files absent
- RBRR fields blank
- hallmark pins blank in every nameplate regime
- depot-scoped fields blank in every vessel regime

**Sequence body** (per RELEASE.md L63–69):
- pristine-lifecycle fixture (gate + simple + credentialed sections — depends on BBAAK landing)
- canonical infrastructure setup — levy canonical depot (`rbw-dL`), mantle canonical governor (`rbw-aM`), invest canonical retriever (`rbw-arI` per BBAAN), invest canonical director (`rbw-adI` per BBAAN), IAM propagation wait
- supply chain — inscribe reliquary (`rbw-dI`), ordain all hallmarks (`rbw-fO` per vessel)
- crucible suite — charge/run-fixture/quench × {tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml}, sequential, per-fixture quench-after to avoid cross-fixture contamination
- cleanup — abjure all hallmarks (`rbw-fA`), divest operational SAs (`rbw-arD`, `rbw-adD` per BBAAN), unmake depot (`rbw-dU`); governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN's settled posture

**Exit semantics**: green tally on success; operator-actionable diagnostic on failure naming the marshal-zero → pristine sequence per RELEASE.md L74.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- Reuse existing tabtargets exclusively — no inline cloud-mutating logic
- Real-time review at each step before commit
- Wrap on script-exists + parses + invocation shapes correct; do **not** wrap on "ran end-to-end" — that is the first-run pace's wrap criterion

## Cross-pace dependencies

- BBAAK landed (credentialed fixture cases available)
- BBAAN landed (cult-verb tabtargets `rbw-arI`, `rbw-adI`, `rbw-arD`, `rbw-adD`, `rbw-arr`, `rbw-adr`)

## Out of scope

- First end-to-end run against fresh cloud state (sibling pace)
- Modifications to existing tabtargets to support orchestration — surface as separate paces if needed
- Phase-skip flags or partial re-run flags (single-shot ceremony per RELEASE.md L10–11)
- Pristine-passed sentinel artifact (BBAAH territory)
- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (BBAAH)

input.content:
# jjezs_slate rbw-tP-first-run

## Character

First end-to-end execution of `rbw-tP` against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here, not in construction.

**Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until `rbw-tP` runs green end-to-end.

Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.

## Cost

~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from infrastructure setup). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.

## Scope

- Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
- Install RBRAs from secure backup (manual operator step per runbook)
- Run `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`
- Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
- Triage routing per surprise:
  - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
  - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
- Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
- Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
- Per RELEASE.md L10–11 failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from step 2, not patch-and-continue

## Cross-pace dependencies

- ₢BBAAW (rbw-tP-build) landed — the script exists and parses

## Out of scope

- Restoring RBRAs after marshal-zero (manual operator step per runbook)
- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (BBAAH)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios
- Cleanup beyond what `rbw-tP` itself does (cleanup is in the script body, owned by ₢BBAAW)
